lines.text: I'm positioned at the East Side perimeter, near Atwood and Schenk's Corners. We're {{ELAPSED}} in and the neighborhood density is already climbing. Madison-wide kill count is holding at thirty-eight as of twenty-two-fifteen.

Year-over-year comparison: we're tracking about even with last year's twenty-two hundred mark. But the distribution is... different. Capitol Square moved faster. Beltline corridor is quiet, which is unusual.

There's a pattern in the 2159 data — the ten-year mark always had these weird redistributions. Activity would spike in unexpected zones, methods would shift. I'm seeing echoes of that tonight and I don't love it.

Bladed weapons are up fourteen percent from this hour last year. Firearms still dominate, but the economics are showing. People are using what they can afford. That's a datapoint I wish I was wrong about.

There's a group moving northeast through the Atwood grid. I count seven individuals, they're coordinated, they've got vests — tactical-adjacent but not professional. I'm going to relocate and get a better angle on the —

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide count: 38 (22:15). YoY delta: +2. East Side: 12 kills this hour alone.
Capitol Square perimeter breach reported at 21:40. Inner fortifications held. Regional Director Calloway's status: sheltered.
Ten-year anniversary observation (2159 Purge): unusual spatial redistribution pattern detected. Current data mirrors 2159 anomaly. Cause unknown.
PurgeShield Panels — 2168 performance report: 4,200 units deployed Madison metro. Your barricade could be the difference. Call 555-0147.
